summary: Verizon CEO Dan Schulman has now completed the 13,000 layoffs he promised last November, but more could lie ahead.

title: Brazil's Nicnet taps Aprecomm to optimize its fiber gateways
summary: Nicnet, a fiber operator in Brazil, is deploying Aprecomm's AI-assisted customer experience and customer premises equipment management tech, including its Wi-Fi optimization software, on broadband gateways. #pressrelease

title: Optimum eyes virtualization to bridge the FTTP-HFC reliability gap
summary: Optimum has teamed with Harmonic for its first large-scale deployment of a virtual CMTS. 'Virtualization could level the playing field' between HFC and FTTP, says COO Nate Edwards.

title: Starlink D2D use is less than expected, says T-Mobile
summary: T-Mobile revealed Starlink-based direct-to-device service is not used as much as expected since it launched in July last year and rejected the idea of a Starlink MVNO deal.

title: Why the Grain Management spectrum swap with T-Mobile deserves a closer look
summary: Grain wants to meet a terrestrial buildout obligation with a satellite service that, by rule, doesn't satisfy it. The FCC hasn't noticed yet.

title: Rogers to offer voluntary buyouts to 10,000 employees
summary: As part of a bigger cost-cutting initiative, Canada's Rogers is reportedly going to offer voluntary buyouts to about 10,000 employees, or roughly half its workforce.

title: Blaize, Nokia and Datacomm to deliver hybrid AI infrastructure in Indonesia
summary: Indonesia will serve as a reference market with validated deployments providing a blueprint for expansion across Southeast Asia.

title: China Mobile unleashes first robot dog
summary: China Mobile's new companion robot Lingxi can recognize and follow its owner.

title: New Omdia research states that cellular IoT data traffic will reach 218.6 exabytes by 2035
summary: The majority of cellular IoT data traffic will come from the automotive sector, with key use cases including infotainment services such as video and audio streaming, as well as firmware over-the-air updates. #pressrelease

title: T-Mobile's new JVs fixate on fiber
summary: Marking a $2.7 billion investment that will aid a broader convergence strategy, T-Mobile has formed JVs with Oak Hill Capital and Wren House to acquire GoNetspeed, Greenlight Networks and i3 Broadband.

title: T-Mobile signs pair of strategic fiber JVs
summary: T-Mobile has formed strategic fiber joint ventures with Oak Hill Capital and Wren House. #pressrelease

title: T-Mobile and Starlink pitch businesses on blended broadband
summary: The 5G-plus-satellite 'SuperBroadband' bundle from T-Mobile and Starlink promises coverage in every US ZIP code. There's a 36-month commitment attached to that simplicity.
